patch.value: Observed live 2026-07-10: `pm activity --from <ts> --compact --json` returns an envelope containing BOTH `"activity": []` (always empty in compact mode) and `"compact_activity": [...]` (the real rows). Two defects: (1) token waste — a constant dead key ships on every compact query, contrary to the lean-output direction (GH-435 pm-cfed, GH-437 pm-nilh); (2) agent-shape trap — an agent probing `.activity` (or `.entries`) sees a plausible empty array and concludes "no activity" instead of erroring, a silent-wrong-answer class worse than a missing key. Fix direction: compact mode should emit ONE canonical key (either reuse `activity` for the compact rows, or omit the empty array entirely); apply the same single-key rule to the MCP pm_run activity envelope and document the shape in contracts. Related: pm-cfed (lean JSON), pm-nilh (lean default), closed pm-ne67 (introduced --compact).
pm activity --compact --json emits an empty activity:[] decoy key alongside compact_activity
compact --json emits exactly one activity key (no empty decoy array); MCP activity envelope matches; contracts/docs state the compact JSON shape; regression test asserts the empty key is absent

author: codex-root

patch.value: Remove the empty activity decoy from compact JSON and establish one unambiguous activity payload key without breaking alias compatibility.
